problem_statement: Create a multi-workflow agent system that provides users with two distinct development workflows: a structured 4-phase workflow (spec) for complex features requiring planning and architecture, and a direct vibe workflow for rapid iteration and simpler tasks. The system should include cross-platform wrapper scripts for easy agent selection.
goals: Create Python wrapper script (agent) with shebang for Mac/Linux that maps subcommands to agent names
Create Windows batch wrapper script (agent.bat) with same functionality
Add spec agent configuration for 4-phase structured workflow (current orchestrator workflow)
Add vibe agent configuration for direct implement/test loop workflow
Remove custom build and plan agent configurations to use OpenCode defaults
Update AGENTS.md to document both workflows and when to use each
Ensure wrapper scripts launch opencode with appropriate agent selected without passing prompt
constraints: Wrapper scripts must NOT pass any prompt to opencode - user enters prompt in TUI
Spec agent must maintain the existing 4-phase structured workflow from AGENTS.md
Vibe agent must have full tool access for direct implementation
Vibe agent should optionally be able to delegate to subagents
Both wrapper scripts must support subcommands: spec, vibe, build, plan
Documentation must clearly explain when to use each workflow

tech_choices.choice: Python 3 with shebang for Mac/Linux, Batch for Windows
tech_choices.rationale: Python provides cross-platform compatibility and is already likely installed on developer machines. Shebang enables direct execution on Unix systems. Batch file is necessary for Windows compatibility.
OpenCode's native agent configuration system provides the flexibility needed for different workflow patterns

tech_choices.choice: Primary agent with orchestrator prompt and subagent delegation
tech_choices.rationale: The structured workflow requires orchestration between understanding, planning, testing, and implementation phases

tech_choices.choice: Primary agent with full permissions and optional subagent access
tech_choices.rationale: Direct implementation workflow needs full tool access for rapid iteration, with optional delegation for complex tasks

architecture.description: The system consists of two main components: wrapper scripts for easy agent selection, and agent configurations for different workflow patterns. The wrapper scripts provide a simple CLI interface that maps subcommands to agent names, launching opencode with the appropriate agent selected. The agent configurations define two distinct workflows: spec (structured 4-phase) and vibe (direct implementation).

components.name: agent wrapper script (Mac/Linux)
components.responsibility: Provides CLI interface for selecting agents on Unix systems. Parses subcommand, maps to agent name, and launches opencode with --agent flag. Does NOT pass any prompt.

components.name: agent.bat wrapper script (Windows)
components.responsibility: Provides CLI interface for selecting agents on Windows systems. Parses subcommand, maps to agent name, and launches opencode with --agent flag. Does NOT pass any prompt.

components.name: spec agent configuration
components.responsibility: Orchestrates 4-phase structured workflow: Understand (direct with user), Plan (with @planner), Write Tests (with @test-writer), Implement (with @implementer). Uses orchestrator prompt and delegates to subagents.

components.name: vibe agent configuration
components.responsibility: Provides direct implementation workflow. Engages user to understand requirements, plans directly with user (no plan file), implements and tests in a loop. Has full tool access and can optionally delegate to subagents.

components.name: updated AGENTS.md
components.responsibility: Documents both workflows, wrapper script usage, and guidance on when to use each workflow.

test_strategy.approach: Manual testing of wrapper scripts and agent configurations. Since these are configuration and wrapper files, testing focuses on verification that scripts execute correctly and agents launch with expected behavior.

test_cases.description: Verify agent script executes with valid subcommand (spec)
Verify agent script executes with valid subcommand (vibe)
Verify agent script handles invalid subcommand gracefully
Verify agent.bat executes with valid subcommand (spec)
Verify agent.bat executes with valid subcommand (vibe)
Verify spec agent launches with orchestrator prompt
Verify vibe agent launches with full permissions
Verify build and plan agents use OpenCode defaults

file_changes.description: Python wrapper script with shebang for Mac/Linux. Maps subcommands (spec, vibe, build, plan) to agent names and launches opencode with --agent flag. Does NOT pass any prompt.
Windows batch wrapper script. Maps subcommands (spec, vibe, build, plan) to agent names and launches opencode with --agent flag. Does NOT pass any prompt.
Add spec agent configuration (4-phase structured workflow), add vibe agent configuration (direct implementation workflow), remove custom build and plan configurations.
Update documentation to include wrapper script usage, spec workflow documentation, vibe workflow documentation, and guidance on when to use each workflow.
